title: pungent shattering brazilian pork tenderloin poach
description: Weeknight friendly Brazilian pork tenderloin dish with a hint of spice and a satisfying crunch. This recipe is perfect for a quick and easy dinner.
instructions: Season the pork tenderloin with salt, black pepper, cumin, and paprika.
Heat a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Sear the pork tenderloin on all sides until browned, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and roast for 12-15 minutes, or until the pork reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C).
While the pork is cooking, heat the orange juice in a small saucepan over medium heat until slightly reduced.
Add the sliced onion to the skillet with the pork and cook until caramelized, about 5-7 minutes.
Remove the pork from the oven and let rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
To serve, place the sliced pork on a plate and spoon some of the caramelized onion and orange juice sauce over the top.
If desired, sprinkle with green chili flake for an extra kick.
